body.title: Shim idle-sleeps a device via bus ask ('whatcha workin on?'); busy/dead disambiguated by PID
body.intention: I intend that the front-door shim shuts down an idle device (freeing its slot) after a timeout, detected by a bus ask, while never shutting down a busy one.
body.description:
**Blocked on: T-shim-frontdoor-on-groundloop** (needs the front-door that owns device lifecycle) and reads the lease (T-shim-lease-instance-numbers).

**What to build:** on the front-door's periodic tick, the shim BUS-ASKS each live instance 'whatcha workin' on?' and decides:
- reply 'idle past the timeout' -> shim shuts the device down (idle-sleep) and RELEASES its slot;
- NO reply + PID alive -> busy heads-down mid-ticket (services mailbox only when idle) -> leave it;
- NO reply + PID dead -> crashed -> release the slot.
The idle timeout is a CONFIG default (~2 min sketch), tunable.

**Completion criteria:** an idle instance that replies 'idle > timeout' is shut down and its slot freed; a busy instance (no reply, PID alive) is left running; a dead PID (no reply) has its slot reclaimed.

**Test plan (real red->green):** unit/integration — stub an instance's bus reply as 'idle Nmin' -> assert shutdown+release; stub no-reply with a live PID -> assert left alone; no-reply with a dead PID -> assert slot freed. Red before the idle-poll exists; green after.

**Validation (runtime):** a DS.1 left idle past the timeout disappears (folder freed, slot reusable) while a DS.0 working a ticket stays up. Observable via the idle-sleep log + the freed slot.

**Logging:** log.info('idle-sleep: shutting down %s (idle %ds)', instance_name, idle) ; log.warning('idle-poll: %s unreachable, PID %s dead -> reclaiming', instance_name, pid).

**Observable via:** log.info('idle-sleep: shutting down DS.1') + slot 1 becoming free.

**Affected files (indicative):** unseen_university/devices/<worker>/shim.py (poll + decision), the front-door tick loop, the bus control verb the instance answers.

**Rollback:** git revert — devices stay up until explicitly stopped (no auto-sleep). No migration.

**audit-emphasis:** needs-deep-smell (the busy-vs-dead disambiguation is the load-bearing correctness point — a wrong call kills a working instance).

**Scope boundary:** the idle-detect + sleep + slot-release; leasing + pool cap are separate tickets.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

---

**BUILD SPEC (resolved 2026-07-02, advisor-reviewed — INSTANCE owns the idle decision; fire-and-forget, no reply, race-free by construction).** Refines D-worker-pool-leasing's "shim asks, reply drives decision" (divergence recorded in that decision): a reply-then-kill gap would let a new dispatch arrive and be killed mid-ticket. Instead the instance self-exits only when idle; because the worker listener is SYNCHRONOUS, a busy instance is blocked in `_handle_dispatch` and physically cannot process the quit — "never kill a busy instance" holds by construction.

**File 1 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/worker_listener.py`:**
- `import time`, `import os`, `import signal`. Constructor gains `on_idle_shutdown=None` (injected Callable[[], None], like `on_bus_failure`) and reads `self._idle_timeout_s = float(os.environ.get("DICKSIMNEL_IDLE_TIMEOUT_S", "120"))`.
- Track idle: set `self._last_active = time.monotonic()` in `start()`, and refresh it whenever a ticket completes (right where `_active_ticket` is set back to None at the end of `_handle_dispatch`).
- `_poll_once` DRAIN-SAFE handling: iterate the fetched batch tracking `dispatch_seen` and `quit_seen`. Handle `kind=="dispatch"` as today (this sets/works the ticket). Keep `kind in ("halt","priority")` as today. For `kind=="quit_if_idle"`: do NOT act inside the loop — just set `quit_seen=True` (capture the payload's `idle_timeout` if present). AFTER the loop: if `quit_seen and not dispatch_seen and self._device._active_ticket is None and (time.monotonic() - self._last_active) >= self._idle_timeout_s`: `log.info("idle-sleep: %s idle %.0fs — self-exiting", <instance>, idle)` and call `self._on_idle_shutdown()` if set. (The `not dispatch_seen` guard closes the co-arrival message-loss trap: a quit that co-arrives with a dispatch in one fetch_unseen batch is ignored — the instance just worked, so it is not idle. Granny escalates un-acked work on dispatch_timeout, so nothing is lost either way, but do not needlessly trigger it.)

**File 2 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/shim.py`:** when constructing `DickSimnelWorkerListener` in `start()`, pass `on_idle_shutdown=self._on_idle_shutdown`. Add method `_on_idle_shutdown(self)`: `log.info` + `os.kill(os.getpid(), signal.SIGTERM)` — the existing `__main__` SIGTERM handler already calls `device._shim.stop()` then `sys.exit(0)`, so the process exits cleanly (no in-flight ticket at idle-exit). Add `import os, signal`.

**File 3 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/devices/pool.py`:** add `cull_dead(self) -> list[int]` — iterate in-memory slots; for each non-None slot call `self._liveness(pid, create_time)`; set dead ones to None (return the reclaimed indices); trim trailing Nones; persist. UNLIKE `rebuild()`, this operates in-memory and PRESERVES live slots' Popen handles (rebuild drops them by reloading from disk). This is the periodic dead-reclaim primitive.

**File 4 (MODIFY) `unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/frontdoor.py`:** add a periodic idle-sweeper as a SEPARATE daemon thread started in `start()` (do NOT fold into the idle_wait loop — different cadence). `IDLE_POLL_INTERVAL_S = float(os.environ.get("DICKSIMNEL_IDLE_POLL_INTERVAL_S", "120"))`. The sweeper: `while not self._stop.is_set(): self._idle_sweep(); self._stop.wait(IDLE_POLL_INTERVAL_S)`. `_idle_sweep()`: (a) for each `n in self._pool.taken()`, FIRE-AND-FORGET a `quit_if_idle` envelope to mailbox `f"dicksimnel.{n}"` via `self._bus.append(...)` with payload `{"kind":"quit_if_idle","idle_timeout": <DICKSIMNEL_IDLE_TIMEOUT_S default 120>}` and `from_device="dicksimnel-frontdoor"` (APPEND only — never fetch dicksimnel.0; single-consumer invariant preserved); (b) `self._pool.cull_dead()` to reclaim dead slots. Fail-soft: wrap each in try/except + log.warning. `stop()` already sets `self._stop`, which ends the sweeper. Guard the sweeper if `self._bus is None`.

**PROOF (gates close — anchor = "a BUSY instance is NEVER shut down", the audit's load-bearing failure mode): `tests/test_idle_sleep.py::test_busy_instance_never_self_exits`.** Construct a `DickSimnelWorkerListener` with a spy `on_idle_shutdown` and a fake device whose `_active_ticket = "T-busy"` (busy). Feed a `quit_if_idle` envelope through `_poll_once` (fake bus returns [that envelope]). Assert `on_idle_shutdown` was NOT called (a busy instance survives the quit). Stub the quit-handler to call `on_idle_shutdown` unconditionally (ignoring the `_active_ticket`/idle guard) → busy-survives test RED → add the `_active_ticket is None and idle>=timeout` guard → GREEN. Killing a working instance is the failure mode; the proof proves its ABSENCE. Companion non-proof tests (same file): idle instance (`_active_ticket None`, `_last_active` far in the past) → on_idle_shutdown CALLED; quit co-arriving with a dispatch in one batch → dispatch handled, on_idle_shutdown NOT called; `pool.cull_dead()` reclaims a dead-PID slot (injected liveness) and preserves a live one's handle.

**Scope boundary:** idle-detect + self-exit + dead-slot reclaim only. Per-instance mailbox routing (slot n>0 uses `dicksimnel.<n>`; today only slot 0 = `dicksimnel.0` exists) and multi-instance spawn = T-granny-wake. Do NOT add reply mailboxes or a status round-trip (the whole point of the refinement is to delete them).

**Operational:** re-run `tests/test_dicksimnel_frontdoor.py`, `tests/test_instance_pool.py`, `tests/test_pool_semantics.py`, `tests/test_worker_instance_identity.py`, and the DS listener tests (`tests/dicksimnel/`, `tests/inference/test_dicksimnel_toolloop.py`) — the listener + shim + pool changes must not regress.

**Affected files (concrete):** MODIFY worker_listener.py, shim.py, pool.py, frontdoor.py; NEW tests/test_idle_sleep.py.
body.result: Idle-sleep shipped as a race-free refinement (instance owns the idle decision, fire-and-forget — divergence from D-worker-pool-leasing's 'reply drives decision' recorded in that decision). Front-door sweeper thread fires quit_if_idle to each live slot + runs pool.cull_dead() (in-memory dead-reclaim preserving live handles) every IDLE_POLL_INTERVAL; never reads dicksimnel.0 (append-only, single-consumer preserved). The synchronous listener self-exits ONLY when idle (_active_ticket None, idle>=timeout, no dispatch co-arrived in batch) via a shim SIGTERM callback; a busy instance is blocked in _handle_dispatch and physically can't process the quit. Proven: a busy instance is NEVER shut down (red without the busy-guard, green with it). Drain-safe batch handling closes the quit+dispatch co-arrival message-loss trap. Scope-held: per-instance mailbox + multi-spawn = T-granny-wake.
